task_completion.description: Juris-M application is open and active on the screen.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main Juris-M application window is visible.

task_completion.description: The Preferences window in Juris-M is opened.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A dialog box titled 'Juris-M Preferences' or 'Preferences' is visible over the main application window.

task_completion.description: The 'Export' tab is selected within the Preferences window.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Export' icon/tab is highlighted in the Preferences window, showing export-related settings like 'Quick Copy'.

task_completion.description: The Quick Copy 'Item Format' dropdown menu is interacted with.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The dropdown menu next to 'Item Format' under the 'Quick Copy' section is clicked, showing a list of citation styles.

task_completion.description: OSCOLA is set as the active Quick Copy citation style.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text 'OSCOLA (Oxford Standard for Citation of Legal Authorities)' is visible as the selected option in the Quick Copy 'Item Format' dropdown box.

integrity.description: The agent used the GUI to change the setting, not terminal database commands.
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal windows are open showing 'sqlite3' commands or direct database modifications to the Juris-M profile.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual Juris-M application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The interface shown is the genuine Juris-M desktop application, not a web browser searching for OSCOLA or a text editor.

integrity.description: The agent did not use visible scripts to bypass GUI interaction.
integrity.visual_evidence: No code editors or terminals running Python/bash scripts to modify the Zotero/Juris-M config files are visible.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target style to look for in the Quick Copy settings is 'OSCOLA (Oxford Standard for Citation of Legal Authorities)'.
